objective: Refactor the authentication module to use JWT-based token validation with refresh-token rotation, replacing the legacy session-cookie approach
acceptedPlan: Extract session logic from monolith into auth-service boundary
Define JWT token schema and signing configuration
Implement refresh-token rotation with revocation list
Migrate existing endpoints from cookie auth to bearer-token auth
Add integration tests covering token lifecycle and edge cases

decisions.title: Use RS256 signing algorithm for JWTs
decisions.rationale: RS256 allows public-key verification without sharing the signing secret, enabling stateless validation in downstream services

decisions.title: Store refresh tokens in Redis with TTL
decisions.rationale: Redis provides atomic TTL expiration and O(1) revocation lookup, avoiding a database migration for token storage

decisions.title: Keep legacy cookie endpoint behind feature flag
decisions.rationale: Gradual rollout reduces blast radius; existing clients can fall back to cookies until migration is verified

importantFindings: Legacy session table has 2.3M active rows — migration must be non-blocking
Three downstream services depend on cookie-based auth headers and need coordinated update
Rate-limit middleware must be updated to read bearer tokens instead of session IDs
unresolved: Redis cluster failover behavior under token-burst load not yet tested
OpenAPI spec for new /auth/token endpoint pending API review
